item.title: BofA Sees Record May for High-Grade Sales, Topping $190 Billion
item.description: Bank of America Corp. expects this May to be the busiest since the Covid-19 pandemic for US investment-grade corporate bond sales, as companies rush to raise capital before rising oil prices and a strong US economy can weigh on borrowing costs.

item.title: Saint Augustine’s University Files for Bankruptcy After Taking Out 24% Loan
item.description: Saint Augustine’s University, one of the oldest historically Black colleges in the country, has filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection.

item.title: Argentina Hunts for LNG Imports for Winter in War-Wrecked Market
item.description: Argentina is looking to purchase liquefied natural gas cargoes for its upcoming winter heating season, adding demand to a market already squeezed by the war in Iran.

item.title: Natixis Sells Down Stake in US Boutique Adviser Solomon Partners
item.description: Natixis SA has sold down its majority stake in Solomon Partners, in a move that hands control back to the New York-based advisory firm’s management.

item.title: US Ends Investigation Into Claims WhatsApp Chats Aren’t Private
item.description: A US agency abruptly closed an investigation into allegations that Meta Platforms Inc. can access encrypted WhatsApp messages, said two people familiar with the matter, ending a law enforcement inquiry into claims that challenge how the company markets the chat service’s privacy.

item.title: Mamdani Pushes to Shrink NY Hedge-Fund, Private-Equity Tax Break
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is delaying the city’s $127 billion budget as he pressures the state to limit a tax credit used by hedge funds, private equity firms and other businesses.

item.title: OpenAI Trial Judge Lectures Musk, Altman on Trading Social Media Barbs
item.description: The judge overseeing a high-profile court case between Elon Musk and OpenAI has asked the tech executives involved in the legal battle to refrain from posting too much on social media, one day after the world’s richest person made a series of jabs at the ChatGPT maker’s leadership on X.

item.title: Amazon Launches AI Productivity Software for Office Workers
item.description: Amazon.com Inc.’s cloud unit, best known for providing technology infrastructure to corporations, is looking to sell AI-powered productivity software for the office.

item.title: Blue Apron’s Lone Meal Maker Files Bankruptcy After Food Recalls
item.description: FreshRealm, the exclusive maker of meal kits for Blue Apron, filed for bankruptcy with plans to sell its assets and settle a food-recall dispute that helped pressure the company into seeking court protection from creditors.

item.title: Spain Cement Demand Hits 15-Year High for Quarter After Rains
item.description: Spain’s cement consumption hit a 15-year first-quarter high, after a March rebound more than offset a slump caused by historically heavy rains.

item.title: Ryanair CEO Is ‘Less Concerned’ Europe Will Run Out of Jet Fuel
item.description: Ryanair Holdings Plc Chief Executive Officer Michael O’Leary is “increasingly less concerned” about Europe facing a jet-fuel shortage in the coming months, though summer ticket sales may take a hit because of the uncertainty surrounding the Iran war.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Dimon Warns Again on Risks of Credit Downturn
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s Jamie Dimon again cautioned that a credit market downturn could be worse than expected, even after his firm and Wall Street rivals posted a banner quarter in which loan portfolios held up.

item.title: Citadel Books More US Rockies Gas Transport on Key Pipeline
item.description: Citadel Energy Marketing, the merchant trading arm of Ken Griffin’s Citadel, has expanded its natural gas pipeline transportation capacity on a key interstate conduit from the Rocky Mountains to Arizona by 37%, positioning itself ahead of anticipated growth in Southwest gas demand.

item.title: Polymarket Seeks CFTC Blessing to Bring Main Exchange Back to US
item.description: Polymarket is taking steps to try to bring its main exchange back to the US, according to people familiar with the matter, potentially supercharging an already fast-growing industry by letting Americans access the platform.

item.title: Airtel Eyes $2 Billion London IPO of Mobile Money Unit
item.description: Airtel Africa Plc’s initial public offering of its mobile money business could raise between $1.5 billion and $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Hungary Central Bank Chief Signals Caution on Magyar’s Euro Plan
item.description: Hungary’s central bank governor urged caution on the new government’s plan to adopt the euro, saying that a rushed process could lead to unintended negative consequences for the economy.

item.title: Hungarian Nuclear Plant to Cut Block 2 Capacity 50% for Repairs
item.description: Hungary’s nuclear energy authority said output at one block of the country’s sole atomic power station will be cut by 50% from April 29 for repairs on a generator support system.

item.title: OpenAI Hits Back at Growth Fears, Says ‘Firing on All Cylinders’
item.description: OpenAI pushed back against concerns over its sales growth on Tuesday, saying its consumer and enterprise businesses are “firing on all cylinders” despite a report about the AI startup missing internal targets.

item.title: EU’s Von der Leyen to Hold Meeting with Chip and Tech Firms
item.description: The European Union executive’s arm will meet with companies from the technology and semiconductor sector this week amid concerns from business that the bloc’s competitiveness drive is falling short.

item.title: Columbia Considers $485 Million Bond Sale for Capital Projects
item.description: Columbia University is planning to tap the bond market next month for capital projects.

item.title: Cosan’s Compass Seeks to End 5-Year Brazil IPO Drought
item.description: Compass Gas e Energia SA is seeking to raise as much as 3.1 billion reais ($622 million) in what would be the first initial public offering in Brazilian stock markets in almost five years.

item.title: US Consumer Confidence Unexpectedly Climbs on Jobs Outlook
item.description: US consumer confidence unexpectedly edged up in April to the highest this year as Americans grew more hopeful about the outlook for the labor market.

item.title: South Africa Extends Tax Cuts on Fuel Price to Offset War Impact
item.description: South Africa extended cuts to fuel taxes as the prolonged conflict in the Middle East threatens to raise domestic gasoline and diesel prices even further.

item.title: T-Mobile Invests in Two Partnerships to Expand US Internet Access
item.description: T-Mobile US Inc. is creating two joint ventures in fiber in an effort to bring high-speed internet to more US users.

item.title: World Bank Sees Commodity Prices Hitting Four-Year High in 2026
item.description: Global commodity prices are set to increase this year to the highest since 2022 as the war in Iran continues to disrupt key industrial supplies like oil and metals, the World Bank Group said.

item.title: Barclays Eyes Over 50% Recovery on MFS as Asset Hunt Uncertain
item.description: Barclays Plc hopes to get back much of the £500 million ($674 million) it lent to Market Financial Solutions Ltd. even as insolvency officials overseeing the now-collapsed mortgage lender remain uncertain on how much can be recovered.

item.title: Data Center-Linked Bonds Slide as OpenAI Report Fuels Worries
item.description: US corporate bonds linked to data-center firms fell Tuesday after the Wall Street Journal reported that OpenAI recently failed to meet its own goals for new user acquisition and sales, fueling internal concerns that the company may struggle to support its spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Tech Companies to Lead Earnings Growth as Chip Strength Shines
item.description: US technology companies are emerging as the biggest gainers this earnings season as chipmakers reap the benefits of Big Tech’s unprecedented spending on artificial intelligence.

item.title: Nvidia-Supplier Victory Giant’s Sales Surge on Solid AI Demand
item.description: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. reported a 28% increase in quarterly sales on stronger demand for printed circuit boards critical for development of AI servers.

item.title: UAE to Leave OPEC and OPEC+ in May to Pursue New Strategy
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has decided to leave the OPEC and OPEC+ oil producer groups as it plans a strategic realignment in the wake of the Iran war.
